mnemonics: Dotted Decimals Determine Destinations (IPv4 addresses are written in dotted-decimal notation and determine where data is sent)
keyPoints: An IPv4 address is a 32-bit numerical identifier for a network interface
The address is divided into a network portion and a host portion using a subnet mask
IPv4 uses classful (A, B, C, D, E) and classless (CIDR) addressing schemes
commonMistakes: Confusing public and private IP address ranges (e.g., 192.168.x.x is private)
Miscalculating subnet masks and resulting network ranges
Forgetting that network and broadcast addresses cannot be assigned to hosts
